notes: Complex generation fixtures target the current public plan-skill and generate-skill command path with richer input sections.
Fixture workspaces are marker-owned, repository-local, and removed after each fixture case.

fixtures.target_rule: generate-skill creates a richer multi-file skill scaffold from public complex input and validates cross-file references plus maintenance surfaces.
known_limitations: This is one complex generation regression and does not prove broad generator coverage.

fixtures.target_rule: generate-skill blocks malformed complex blueprint input with a public-safe blocker.
known_limitations: Covers one malformed complex blueprint field.

fixtures.target_rule: fixture validation fails when generated required files are removed and generated fixture manifest content is invalid.
known_limitations: Corruption is fixture-injected after generation to prove downstream validation catches malformed generated output.

skipped_checks: These fixtures do not claim package publication, release readiness, external service integration, or semantic acceptance of generated skills.
These fixtures do not replace generator unit tests for every possible rich input, unsupported path, overwrite-protection, or generated content corruption shape.
residual_risk: Future generator contract changes may require refreshing expected generated content fragments and fixture output evidence.
The generated scaffold remains a draft artifact and still needs separate review before any accepted status can be claimed.
claim_boundary: This complex generation fixture manifest covers only the listed public generate-skill fixture cases and current local files. It does not prove runtime checker execution beyond this fixture invocation, broad fixture coverage, CLI checks, tests, suite automation, package publication, code-contract validation, release readiness, or future AI behavior without separate current evidence.
